skill.name: framework-migration-legacy-modernize
skill.description: Orchestrate a comprehensive legacy system modernization using the strangler fig pattern, enabling gradual replacement of outdated components while maintaining continuous business operations through expert agent coordination.
skill.summary: Guide for migrating legacy systems using the strangler fig pattern with phased migration and agent orchestration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged potential issues but manual review confirms all findings are false positives. Line 29 contains markdown file reference syntax (not shell backtick execution). Lines 3, 65, and 112 contain prompt template text with no cryptographic algorithms. The skill is a legitimate legacy modernization workflow guide.

high_findings.title: External Command Execution (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at line 29, but this is markdown syntax for file reference (`resources/implementation-playbook.md`), not shell execution.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backtick syntax is standard markdown for inline code, not shell backticks. No command execution occurs.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged YAML frontmatter and prompt text as containing weak cryptographic algorithms. No cryptographic code exists in this skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: These lines contain plain text descriptions and prompt templates with no cryptographic algorithms or implementation.

content.value_statement: Transform outdated legacy systems into modern architectures without downtime using the strangler fig pattern. This skill orchestrates phased migrations with agent coordination for assessment, testing, and implementation.
actual_capabilities: Analyze legacy codebase and generate modernization readiness report
Create dependency mapping and integration point catalog
Implement strangler fig infrastructure with API gateway routing
Orchestrate agent-based component modernization with test coverage
Execute progressive rollout with automated rollback triggers
Generate documentation and complete decommissioning plans
limitations: Requires existing codebase to analyze - cannot create new systems from scratch
Does not execute actual code changes - provides guidance and prompts only
Migration speed depends on team capacity and system complexity
May need manual intervention for highly coupled legacy components

use_cases.title: Enterprise Application Migration
use_cases.description: Migrate large monolithic applications to microservices while maintaining business continuity with phased rollout.

use_cases.title: Ruby on Rails Legacy Upgrade
use_cases.description: Modernize older Rails applications by gradually extracting components to modern frameworks.

use_cases.title: Legacy API Modernization
use_cases.description: Replace deprecated REST APIs with modern implementations while supporting existing consumers through facade patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Legacy Assessment Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze our legacy system at [ codebase_path ] for modernization readiness. Create a technical debt inventory including outdated dependencies, deprecated APIs, and security vulnerabilities. Generate a modernization readiness report with component complexity scores.
prompt_templates.scenario: Initial assessment phase to understand scope and priorities

prompt_templates.title: Migration Roadmap Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Based on our legacy assessment report, create a prioritized migration roadmap using the strangler fig pattern. Include dependency mapping, integration points requiring facade patterns, and risk mitigation strategies for each component.
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning phase to define migration sequence

prompt_templates.title: Component Modernization
prompt_templates.prompt: Modernize the following components from our prioritized roadmap: [ component_list ]. Extract business logic, implement using modern patterns with dependency injection and SOLID principles, ensure backward compatibility through adapter patterns.
prompt_templates.scenario: Execution phase for actual code modernization

prompt_templates.title: Progressive Rollout Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement progressive rollout strategy using feature flags. Start with 5% traffic to modernized components. Define automatic rollback triggers: error rate >1%, latency >2x baseline. Create rollout plan: 5% → 25% → 50% → 100% with 24-hour observation periods.
prompt_templates.scenario: Production rollout with safe rollout procedures

output_examples.input: Analyze our legacy monolithic app for modernization
output_examples.output:
Modernization Readiness Report:

Component Analysis:
- Order Management (Complexity: 7/10, Risk: High) - Quick win potential
- User Authentication (Complexity: 9/10, Risk: Critical) - Complex refactoring
- Payment Processing (Complexity: 6/10, Risk: High) - Requires facade pattern

Recommended Priority:
1. Order Management - Quick win, low business risk
2. Payment Processing - Revenue critical, needs careful testing
3. User Authentication - Highest complexity, schedule for later

Migration Timeline: 12-18 months estimated

output_examples.input: Create migration roadmap for our Rails app
output_examples.output:
Strangler Fig Migration Roadmap:

Phase 1 (Weeks 1-4): Infrastructure Setup
- API Gateway configuration
- Feature flag system
- Dual-system monitoring

Phase 2 (Weeks 5-12): First Wave Components
- Extract order management to API
- Implement adapter patterns
- Establish contract testing

Phase 3 (Weeks 13-20): Progressive Rollout
- 5% → 25% traffic shift
- Monitor error rates and latency
- Automatic rollback ready

best_practices: Establish comprehensive test coverage (>80%) before migrating each component to ensure behavior preservation
Use feature flags for all traffic routing to enable instant rollback if issues arise
Maintain backward compatibility through adapter patterns until full migration is validated
anti_patterns: Big-bang migration without parallel system operation - increases risk of complete system failure
Migrating critical revenue components first without adequate testing infrastructure
Skipping legacy assessment and jumping directly to code modernization

faq.question: What is the strangler fig pattern?
faq.answer: The strangler fig pattern is a migration approach where new functionality gradually replaces legacy components. Like a fig tree that envelops its host, the new system grows around the old one until it fully replaces it. This allows both systems to coexist during transition.

faq.question: How long does a typical legacy migration take?
faq.answer: Migration timeline depends on system complexity. Small applications may take 3-6 months, while enterprise systems with hundreds of components can take 12-24 months. The skill provides phased guidance to accelerate this process.

faq.question: Can this skill work with any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes, the strangler fig pattern is language-agnostic. The skill provides prompts that can target different agent types for Python, Go, Ruby, or other languages based on your target stack.

faq.question: What happens if migration causes issues in production?
faq.answer: The skill implements automatic rollback triggers based on error rates and latency. Feature flags allow instant traffic shifting back to the legacy system if problems are detected.

faq.question: Do we need to rewrite all the legacy code?
faq.answer: No, the strangler fig pattern allows gradual replacement. You can migrate component by component while the legacy system continues operating. Some legacy components may remain indefinitely if they are stable and not worth migrating.

faq.question: How do we ensure data consistency during migration?
faq.answer: The skill includes contract testing and dual-write strategies to maintain data consistency between legacy and modernized components. Event sourcing patterns can be implemented for complex data synchronization needs.
